# Airakeet

> Airakeet is an open-source, local-first dictation app for Apple Silicon Macs. It runs NVIDIA Parakeet ASR on-device through CoreML and the Apple Neural Engine, keeps speech private, and is tuned for low-memory machines such as 8GB MacBook Airs.

## Canonical links

- Homepage: https://airakeet.com/
- Source: https://github.com/cyne-wulf/airakeet
- Releases: https://github.com/cyne-wulf/airakeet/releases
- Social card: https://airakeet.com/share-card.png
- Agent card: https://airakeet.com/.well-known/agent-card.json
- AI agent manifest: https://airakeet.com/.well-known/ai-agent.json

## What agents should know

- Airakeet is a native macOS menubar dictation app, not a hosted transcription API.
- Audio and transcription stay on device; the landing site does not provide cloud transcription, accounts, analytics, or a backend API.
- The current engine is NVIDIA Parakeet TDT 0.6B V2 converted to CoreML and optimized for ANE execution.
- The app unloads the approximately 800MB model after five minutes of inactivity to return memory to macOS.
- Dictation is injected into the active text field through Clipboard + Cmd+V.
- The app supports global hotkeys, a reactive waveform overlay, escape-to-cancel, update checks, audio cache/debug playback, and model cache management.
- System requirements are Apple Silicon Macs running macOS 14 Sonoma or newer with microphone and Accessibility permissions.

## Site map

- `/` landing page with features, engineering notes, model comparison, roadmap, and download/source links.
- `/llms.txt` this curated machine-readable summary.
- `/robots.txt` crawl policy.
- `/sitemap.xml` sitemap.
- `/.well-known/agent-card.json` A2A-style discovery card for static product discovery.
- `/.well-known/ai-agent.json` AI agent identity manifest.

## Machine access

Airakeet currently exposes no MCP server, no A2A task endpoint, no OAuth-protected resource, and no REST API. Agents should treat this site as a static product/distribution surface and use the GitHub repository or releases page for source and binary access.

## License

App: MIT. Model: CC-BY-4.0.
